channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned deadly tornadoes. Forecasters warned that rivers in some places would continue to rise for days.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Majority of Americans uneasy over Trump's economic plan: WSJ poll
item.description: Fifty-four percent of voters are against the president’s levies on imported goods while 42 percent of survey respondents said they supported the measure.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Carmelo Anthony and Sue Bird highlight Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Not even 24 hours after his party lost a key Wisconsin race and underperformed in Florida, President Donald Trump followed the playbook that has defined his political career: He doubled down. Trump’s move on Wednesday to place stiff new tariffs on imports from nearly all U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israeli troops deploy to a new security corridor across southern Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli troops deployed to a newly established security corridor across southern Gaza, the military announced Saturday, as pressure on the Hamas militant group increased weeks into the renewed war.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man claims to be ICE agent, threatens to deport victims, deputies say
item.description: A Florida man is accused of pulling a vehicle over, claiming to be an ICE agent, and threatening to deport two victims.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: St. Louis family's prank leads to lobster's prolonged life
item.description: What started off as an April Fool’s prank turned into something more than what Brea Stenzel, her family and a lobster were expecting.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KRON4

item.title: Judge awards $6.6 million to whistleblowers who reported Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton to FBI
item.description: A district court judge has ruled in favor of four whistleblowers who said that Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton retaliated against them for reporting bribery allegations, awarding his employees more than $6 million combined in damages.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy meets European military leaders to plan for a peacekeeping force
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met the leaders of the British and French armed forces in Kyiv Saturday to discuss the potential deployment of a multinational peacekeeping force to Ukraine, despite the reluctance of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Budget calculator: Calculate your monthly budget using the 50/30/20 rule
item.description: The 50/30/20 rule is a popular budgeting technique. Use this calculator to figure out how to spend your money.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors seek 7 years in prison for disgraced ex-US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors are seeking more than seven years in prison for disgraced former U.S. Rep. George Santos after he pleaded guilty to federal fraud and identity theft charges. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: YouTube daredevil arrested by Indian authorities after visiting remote island where US missionary was killed
item.description: A YouTube influencer visiting a remote Indian island, where he allegedly tried to make illegal contact with indigenous people, has been arrested, according to global reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 25 burros die in viral outbreak at Utah corral, BLM says
item.description: An investigation is underway after 25 wild burros died of bronchial pneumonia with an underlying viral infection identified as gammaherpes. The deaths occurred between March 11 and March 21 at the Axtell Off-Range Corral in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shopping spree? Consumers rush to buy goods ahead of Trump tariffs
item.description: President Trump's tariffs are expected to drive up prices, and some consumers are trying to get ahead of the action.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WGN 9

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit if they could find a way to limit their sky-high costs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials with remixed flavors, now exclusively available nationwide at Walmart stores and Walmart.com.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas track meet stabbing suspect told responding officer he 'did it': docs
item.description: The Texas teenager accused of fatally stabbing 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a high school track meet on Wednesday allegedly confessed to the crime to a responding officer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anaheim mayor wants Los Angeles Angels to change their name
item.description: In an open-letter about the Angels future, the Anaheim mayor called for the team to be named for the city it actually represents.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Energy Department considers more than 40 percent of its staff non-essential as layoffs loom
item.description: The Energy Department's portfolio includes both energy technology and innovation as well as nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Columbia must give 30 days' notice before sharing student records with Congress' antisemitism probe
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University must give detained activist Mahmoud Khalil and other students 30 days' notice before handing over any more documents to Congress as it investigates antisemitism on college campuses, a federal judge in New York ruled Friday. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dad's last-minute Christmas gift becomes $7M lotto winner
item.description: A California Lottery scratcher ticket that was given to a man as a gift by his father turned out to be a multimillion-dollar winner.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: California culinary star dies after hiking in Hawaii
item.description: At 26, she was helping to win Michelin stars for Carlsbad's Jeune et Jolie for three years as an executive pastry chef.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, KRON4

item.title: Disgraced former Cardinal Theodore McCarrick has died at age 94
item.description: Theodore McCarrick, a once-powerful Catholic cardinal who was defrocked after an investigation determined he had molested adults and children, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RealPage sues California city officials over rental algorithm ban
item.description: Real estate software company RealPage filed a federal lawsuit Wednesday against Berkeley, California — the latest city to try to block landlords from using algorithms when deciding rents. Officials in many cities claim the practice is anti-competitive and is driving up the price of housing.
item.dc:creator: R.J. RICO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: China to impose a 34% tariff on imports of all US products
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Women's Final Four teams touch the court for shootarounds ahead of tournament
item.description: During press conferences, the teams expressed excitement and an eagerness to fight for their spot in the championship.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired an Iranian scholar, accusing her of refusing to cooperate as it probed allegations that she is involved with a group that the U.S. calls a “sham charity” for a designated terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: An ‘administrative error’ sent a Maryland man to an El Salvador prison, ICE says
item.description: President Donald Trump’ s administration has acknowledged mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison last month, but is arguing against returning him to the United States because of his alleged gang ties and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4
